entry.title: Dr Haithem Afli joins Rinn Artificial Intelligence as Institutional Co-Lead at MTU and Deputy Theme Lead
entry.summary: Dr Haithem Afli has taken on new research leadership roles within Rinn Artificial Intelligence – Research & Innovation in Data Science and AI. He serves as Institutional Co-Lead at MTU, Deputy Theme Lead for Inclusive Language Model & Translation Methods, and Principal Investigator. His work in the centre focuses on inclusive and multilingual language models, translation methods, culturally aware AI, reliable evaluation and human-centred approaches to artificial intelligence.

entry.title: New IWSLT 2026 paper on low-resource cascaded speech translation
entry.summary: A paper by Pournima Sonawane and Haithem Afli was published at IWSLT 2026. The work examines robust cascaded speech translation for Bhojpuri–Hindi and Irish–English, combining Whisper-based ASR with NLLB-200 translation and evaluating routing strategies for low-resource pairs.

entry.title: PTEB accepted at EACL 2026
entry.summary: Manuel Frank and Haithem Afli published PTEB, a dynamic text-embedding evaluation protocol using stochastic paraphrasing at evaluation time, at EACL 2026 (Long Papers).

entry.title: Two papers in Findings of EMNLP 2025
entry.summary: The HAI group contributed GASE (generative augmentation for sentence encoding) and a study on signer dependence in sign language translation evaluation to Findings of EMNLP 2025.

entry.title: Media coverage: “Ireland at AI ‘crossroads’” — Cork Independent
entry.summary: The Cork Independent featured Dr Haithem Afli on Ireland’s opportunity to lead in ethical, human-centred AI — covering AI literacy and inclusion, and the use of AI in precision medicine and healthcare.
